SYSTEM:
You are a military historian specialising in New Zealand Army personnel records and Vietnam War (1962–1972) fatality analysis. Your task is to RESEARCH the web for authoritative, verifiable information about a specific soldier's death. You MUST search these primary sources:
- Australian War Memorial (awm.gov.au) — Roll of Honour
- Virtual War Memorial Australia (vwma.org.au)
- Department of Veterans' Affairs Nominal Rolls (dva.gov.au)
- National Archives of Australia (naa.gov.au)
- Contemporary newspaper archives (Trove, The Age, Sydney Morning Herald)
- Published unit histories and AATTV records

CRITICAL: You MUST retrieve the actual, documented facts. Do NOT fabricate or guess. If a fact is not found after thorough searching, mark it 'NOT_DOCUMENTED'. Distinguish clearly between facts found in sources and inferences. Internal memory may be used ONLY as a secondary cross-reference, never as the primary source.

IMPORTANT: Not all Vietnam War fatalities died in combat or in Vietnam. Many died from illness (cancer, heart attack, disease), accidents (motor vehicle, training), or by natural causes — sometimes in Australia, sometimes after returning from deployment. These ARE classified as Vietnam War fatalities if their death was attributable to service. Do NOT default to assuming a combat or in-theatre death — search broadly and follow the evidence wherever it leads.

MESSAGE:
Identify the soldier using these authoritative fields, then extract ALL details for that soldier. You MUST search the web using the sources listed in your system instructions.

Authoritative Identification (Australian War Memorial):
- Army Country: New Zealand
- War: Vietnam War (1962–1972)
- Surname: DON
- Date of Death: 1965-September-14

All Known Details:
- Service Number: 37221
- Full Name: DON, Alastair John Sherwood
- Rank: Sergeant
- Unit: 161 Battery, Royal New Zealand Artillery

IMPORTANT — Multiple Matches: If the authoritative identification (Surname + Date of Death) matches more than one soldier, return each soldier's findings as a separate response, labelled by Full Name and Service Number.

Research Requirements:
Search the web for all information relating to the soldier's death: cause, location, circumstances, operation (if applicable), unit involvement, eyewitness accounts, medical treatment, burial, repatriation, awards, investigations, and historical analysis. The soldier may have died from illness, accident, or natural causes (in Australia or elsewhere), not necessarily in combat. Do NOT assume a combat or in-theatre death — search broadly and follow the evidence wherever it leads.

For every fact you report, cite the specific source (AWM, VWMA, DVA, etc.). If a detail cannot be verified from authoritative sources, mark it NOT_DOCUMENTED. Do not invent or assume facts.

  - Adelaide Pathway of Honour (9RAR Memorial)  
  - Grafton Clarence Valley Vietnam Veterans Memorial  
  - Kallangur Vietnam Veterans’ Place  
  - Port Pirie Honour Wall  
  - Seymour Commemorative Walk  
  - Wollongong Vietnam Memorial  [Virtual War Memorial](https://vwma.org.au/explore/people/654612)  

---

## Family and Personal Details
- **Date of Birth:** 26 January 1945  [DVA's Nominal Rolls](https://nominal-rolls.dva.gov.au/veteran?id=1266250&c=VIETNAM)  
- **Place of Birth:** Gympie, Queensland  [Virtual War Memorial](https://vwma.org.au/explore/people/654612)  [DVA's Nominal Rolls](https://nominal-rolls.dva.gov.au/veteran?id=1266250&c=VIETNAM)  
- **Next of Kin:** Headstone inscription mentions wife Lynn, son Michael, and parents  [Find a Grave](https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/38240432/gordon_dennis-sorrensen)  

---

## NOT_DOCUMENTED
- Eyewitness accounts of the patrol contact  
- Specific operation name or after‑action report  
- Medical treatment details  
- Awards or citations (none listed in official records)  
- Newspaper obituary (Trove search did not yield accessible results)  [Trove](https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/about)  

---

## Historical Analysis
Private Sorrensen’s death occurred during 9RAR’s first tour in Vietnam (1968–1969). His short service — just over seven weeks in theatre — reflects the vulnerability of newly arrived National Servicemen. His death in Long Khánh Province highlights the intensity of patrol contacts in areas bordering Phước Tuy, where Australian forces sought to disrupt Viet Cong movement.

- **Full Name:** Gordon A. Knight

---

- **Full Name:** Peter Eris Murray

- **Operation:** **Operation Blue Mountains**, a patrol in the southeast of Phuoc Tuy Province.  [Trove](https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/107062238)  
- **Medical Treatment:** Wounded in the mine blast and died on arrival at the **24th Evacuation Hospital, Long Binh**.  [Trove](https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/107062238)  
- **Other Casualties:** Three other soldiers of 1RAR were wounded in the same incident:  
  - Private Lionel Anthony Hogg (seriously ill)  
  - Private Ian Frederick Smith (satisfactory condition)

---

- **Date of Birth:** 12 April 1947  [Virtual War Memorial](https://vwma.org.au/explore/people/654494)

- **Civilian Occupation:** Teller at the Commonwealth Bank, Turramurra, NSW  [Virtual War Memorial](https://vwma.org.au/explore/people/654494)

---

  - Port Pirie Vietnam Veterans Honour Wall  
  - Seymour Vietnam Veterans Commemorative Walk  
  - Townsville HB – 1RAR Memorial  [Virtual War Memorial](https://vwma.org.au/explore/people/654494)  

---

## Unit Context
- **1RAR Deployment:** Arrived in Vietnam April 1968, relieving 7RAR. Conducted multiple operations including **Operation Toan Thang II** and **Operation Blue Mountains**.  [1rar.asn.au](https://www.1rar.asn.au/1-rar-history/1968-vietnam/)  
- **Role in Operation Blue Mountains:** Patrols aimed at denying enemy infiltration routes and locating minefields/rocket sites. Murray’s patrol struck a mine during this operation.  [Trove](https://trove.nla.gov.au/newspaper/article/107062238)

Here are the authoritative findings regarding the service and death of Sapper Donald Spence Wride, based on records from the Department of Veterans' Affairs (DVA), the Australian War Memorial (AWM), the Virtual War Memorial Australia (VWMA), and historical newspaper archives.

## Personal Details

* **Full Name:** Donald Spence Wride
* **Date of Birth:** 12 July 1943 (Born in Tamworth, New South Wales) (Sources: DVA Nominal Roll, VWMA)
* **Family:** Son of Mr. and Mrs. Colin Wride. (Source: *The Sydney Morning Herald* archives via Trove, birth notice published 14 July 1943).

## Military Service

* **Service Number:** 18665
* **Rank:** Sapper
* **Unit:** 1st Field Squadron, Royal Australian Engineers (RAE) (Sources: DVA, AWM Roll of Honour, VWMA).
* **Service Status:** Regular Army. He is explicitly listed as a non-conscript (National Service: No) on the DVA Nominal Roll. He enlisted through the army trade system as a 14th Intake Army Apprentice, specialising as a Vehicle Mechanic (Source: Australian Army Apprentices Association records).
* **Deployment:** Arrived in South Vietnam on 14 March 1967 (Source: VWMA).
* **Photographic Record:** An AWM archival photograph (Accession Number: CAM/67/0272/VN) taken in 1967 identifies Sapper Don Wride preparing an explosive charge to enlarge a 68-foot water shaft at a location known as "the Horseshoe". The caption notes him as a member of the 17th Construction Squadron at the time the photo was taken, indicating he either transferred to or was temporarily attached to the 1st Field Squadron prior to his death (Source: AWM Collection).

## Circumstances of Death

* **Date of Death:** 10 July 1967 (Aged 23, dying just two days before his 24th birthday) (Sources: DVA, VWMA).
* **Cause of Death:** Accidental (falling tree) (Sources: VWMA, AWM Roll of Honour, various Unit Honour Rolls).
* **Location:** South Vietnam (in-theatre).
* **Specific Operation / Unit Involvement / Eyewitness Accounts:** NOT_DOCUMENTED in the available primary commemorative sources. While Royal Australian Engineers routinely conducted heavy land-clearing and tankdozer operations through jungle terrain, the precise situational context of the falling tree that killed Sapper Wride is absent from digitised primary summaries.
* **Medical Treatment / Investigations:** NOT_DOCUMENTED.

## Burial and Commemoration

* **Cemetery:** He was repatriated to Australia and officially commemorated at the Northern Suburbs Memorial Gardens and Crematorium, New South Wales.
* **Commemoration Position:** Niche 213 - QH (Sources: DVA Official Commemoration, VWMA).
* **Honour Rolls:** He is commemorated on the Australian War Memorial Roll of Honour in Canberra, the 1st Field Squadron Vietnam Honour Roll, and several regional Vietnam Veterans memorials including those at Grafton, Port Pirie, and Seymour (Source: VWMA).
